tasks.title: Fix infinite loading screen with error handling and timeout
tasks.description: Refactor the game initialization in main.js and index.html to handle all failure modes (WebGL unavailable, camera denied, MediaPipe load failure) with clear error messages, and add a 15-second global timeout with a retry button.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: 1. Loading screen disappears and game becomes playable when prerequisites are met. 2. Clear error shown when camera access denied. 3. Clear error shown when MediaPipe fails. 4. Clear error shown when WebGL unavailable. 5. 15-second timeout triggers 'failed to load' with retry option.

checklist.description: Add retry button HTML and CSS to index.html
steps: Add `<button id="retry-btn" class="retry-btn">Retry</button>` inside the `#overlay` div after the `.subtitle` div
Add CSS rules for `.retry-btn`, `.retry-btn:hover`, and `.retry-btn.visible` in the existing `<style>` block
Ensure `.retry-btn` has `pointer-events: auto` and is hidden by default (`display: none`)
Verify the button is inside the overlay so it inherits centering

checklist.description: WebGL pre-check shows specific error when WebGL unavailable
steps: Add a WebGL context test before `initRenderer()` call in main.js
If no WebGL context, call `showError()` with a message about WebGL not being supported
Return early from init so no further initialization is attempted

checklist.description: Camera access denial shows specific error message
steps: Replace the camera catch block with specific error name checks: NotAllowedError, NotFoundError, etc.
Each error type gets a distinct, actionable message
Return early from init after showing the error

checklist.description: MediaPipe tracker failure shows specific error message
steps: Wrap `await initTracker(stream)` in a try-catch in main.js
On catch, call `showError()` with a message about head tracking model failure
Return early from init after showing the error

checklist.description: 15-second global timeout triggers failed-to-load message with retry
steps: Refactor init() to use Promise.race with a 15-second timeout
Move initialization logic into a doInit() function
On timeout, show 'Loading timed out' error message with retry button
Clear the timeout on successful init to prevent memory leaks

checklist.description: showError() displays retry button and proper error styling
steps: Update showError() to remove 'hidden' class from overlay
Update showError() to add 'visible' class to retry button
Verify overlay.classList.add('error') sets red text color
Wire retry button click to location.reload()

checklist.description: Normal successful initialization still works — loading screen disappears and game is playable
steps: Verify that when all prerequisites are met, the overlay gets 'hidden' class
Verify score display and leaderboard button become visible
Verify the game loop starts and state transitions to 'playing'
Verify the retry button is NOT visible during/after successful init
Run `docker build -t teeter .` from the project root
Verify the build completes without errors

quality_checklist.description: No duplicate error handling — each failure mode has exactly one handler
steps: Check that WebGL error, camera errors, MediaPipe error, timeout error, and generic catch each have distinct handling
Verify no error condition can trigger two showError() calls

quality_checklist.description: Timeout is properly cleaned up on all code paths
steps: Verify clearTimeout is called in a finally block or equivalent
Verify timeout doesn't fire after successful initialization

quality_checklist.description: Code style matches existing codebase conventions
steps: Verify 2-space indentation throughout changes
Verify camelCase naming for functions and variables
Verify UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants
Verify CSS follows existing patterns in index.html

quality_checklist.description: Retry button is properly hidden during normal flow and visible only on error
steps: Verify retry button starts with display:none
Verify only showError() makes it visible
Verify it has pointer-events:auto so it's clickable despite overlay having pointer-events:none

quality_checklist.description: No modifications to tracker.js, renderer.js, or physics.js
steps: Verify git diff shows no changes to js/tracker.js, js/renderer.js, or js/physics.js

checks.description: Page loads and shows the Teeter loading screen initially, then transitions to the game or shows an appropriate error
steps: Open http://localhost:8080 in a browser
Verify the page loads and shows 'TEETER' title with loading status text
If camera permission is granted and WebGL works, the loading overlay should disappear and the game should become playable with visible score counter and track

checks.description: Error overlay shows a retry button that reloads the page when clicked
steps: Inspect the HTML source to verify the retry button element exists inside #overlay
Verify the retry button has CSS that hides it by default and shows it with the .visible class
Verify the retry button has pointer-events: auto in its CSS

checks.description: WebGL check exists in the initialization code before renderer creation
steps: Read js/main.js and verify there is a WebGL context test (getContext('webgl2') or getContext('webgl')) before initRenderer() is called
Verify that failing the WebGL check calls showError with a message about WebGL

checks.description: Camera error handling distinguishes between denied and not-found errors
steps: Read js/main.js and verify the camera getUserMedia catch block checks err.name for NotAllowedError and NotFoundError
Verify each camera error type has a distinct user-facing message

checks.description: MediaPipe tracker initialization is wrapped in error handling
steps: Read js/main.js and verify initTracker(stream) is inside a try-catch
Verify the catch calls showError with a message about head tracking model failure

checks.description: A 15-second timeout is implemented around the full initialization
steps: Read js/main.js and verify Promise.race is used with a setTimeout of 15000ms
Verify the timeout path calls showError with a timeout-specific message
Verify the timeout is cleared after successful initialization
